source: WMATA GTFS pathways topology (conservative single-platform Street→Mezzanine→Platform ladders)

models.note: One elevator connects the street to the platform. It has no backup — if it is out of service, the station has no step-free route.
models.internalNote: Topology from WMATA GTFS pathways (mode-5 elevator components): Street→Platform: 1 elevator. In-station elevators only — garage/facility elevators are not in the rail GTFS. Slot ids are live UnitNames where observed, synthetic WMATA-<node> otherwise.

models.note: Two elevators connect the street to the mezzanine — either one keeps this leg open. Two elevators connect the mezzanine to the platform — either one keeps this leg open. No single elevator outage removes step-free access.
models.internalNote: Topology from WMATA GTFS pathways (mode-5 elevator components): Street→Mezzanine: 2 elevators, Mezzanine→Platform: 2 elevators. In-station elevators only — garage/facility elevators are not in the rail GTFS. Slot ids are live UnitNames where observed, synthetic WMATA-<node> otherwise.
